deep_dive.title: Shortcut Trajectory Planning for Efficient Offline Reinforcement Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Diffusion-based trajectory planners have shown strong performance in offline reinforcement learning, but their iterative denoising process often incurs high inference cost. Consistency-based planners reduce the number of sampling steps, yet they typically rely on a two-stage teacher--student distillation pipeline that increases training cost and may introduce instability. We propose Shortcut Trajectory Planning (STP), an offline model-based reinforcement learning framework that incorporates shortcut models as efficient trajectory generators. STP trains a conditional shortcut trajectory model in a single stage, supports adjustable one-step and few-step inference through step-size conditioning, and selects candidate plans using a critic augmented with feasibility-aware correction. Across standard D4RL benchmarks, including locomotion, navigation, manipulation, and dexterous control tasks, STP achieves strong performance while simplifying the training pipeline for fast generative planning.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: diffusion-based trajectory planning for offline reinforcement learning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于扩散的轨迹规划用于离线强化学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于扩散的轨迹规划用于离线强化学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于扩散的轨迹规划用于离线强化学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Shortcut Trajectory Planning (STP), a single-stage offline RL framework with diffusion-based trajectory generation and adjustable inference steps.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 离线强化学习中扩散轨迹规划器推理成本高。该文提出STP框架，通过单阶段训练条件快捷轨迹模型，支持一步或多步推理，并使用评论家选择候选计划。实验表明STP在多个离线RL基准上达到与扩散规划器相当的轨迹质量，同时显著降低推理时间和训练复杂度。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: reinforcement learning diffusion policy robot control

deep_dive.title: HALO-WA: Hybrid-Attention Latent-Guided Online Reinforcement Learning for World-Action Models
deep_dive.abstract: World-action (WA) models can generate long-horizon action chunks for general-purpose robotic manipulation, but they remain vulnerable to calibration, perception, and contact-dynamics errors in real-world precision tasks, often failing in the final few millimeters of alignment or insertion. We propose HALO-WA, a hybrid-attention latent-guided online reinforcement learning (RL) framework for WA models, which leverages latent features and action priors from the WA generation process through a lightweight actor-critic adapter to enable fast online adaptation to real deployment errors. HALO-WA introduces a hybrid-attention structure that preserves the temporal consistency of action chunks while reading task-relevant information from WA latents conditioned on visual context and end-stage correction requirements, thereby producing refined action chunks. We validate HALO-WA on four real-world precision manipulation tasks, where it improves the average success rate from 26.4\% for WA-base to 87.1\%, outperforming the strongest baseline by 19.2 percentage points while requiring only 45--75 minutes of online training per task. To facilitate reproducibility, we further conduct supplementary simulation experiments in RoboTwin and release the code at https://github.com/YeanRoot/HALO-WA.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: online reinforcement learning for world-action models in robotic manipulation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向世界-动作模型的在线强化学习用于机器人操作
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向世界-动作模型的在线强化学习用于机器人操作
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向世界-动作模型的在线强化学习用于机器人操作
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: HALO-WA uses hybrid-attention and latent-guided online RL to adapt world-action models to real-world precision tasks, correcting errors in final millimeters.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 世界-动作模型在精密操作中易因校准和接触误差失败。HALO-WA引入混合注意力潜在引导的在线强化学习，通过轻量级actor-critic适配器快速适应真实部署误差。实验证明该方法能在最后几毫米对齐和插入任务中显著提升成功率，为世界-动作模型的可部署性提供了在线适应方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 世界-动作模型在精密操作中易因校准和接触误差失败。HALO-WA引入混合注意力潜在引导的在线强化学习，通过轻量级actor-critic适配器快速适应真实部署误差。实验证明该方法能在最后几毫米对齐和插入任务中显著提升成功率，为世界-动作模型的可部署性提供了在线适应方案。

deep_dive.title: EgoWAM: World Action Models Beyond Pixels with In-the-Wild Egocentric Human Data
deep_dive.abstract: Egocentric human data offers scalable supervision for robot manipulation. However, behavior cloning entangles transferable content like objects, scenes, and task semantics, with non-transferable factors like human morphology, head motion, and behavioral style. We study whether World Action Models (WAMs) provide a better training signal by requiring policies to predict not only actions, but also how the scene evolves. The central question is what world representation best enables human-to-robot transfer. We hypothesize that an effective world target should abstract appearance, capture agent-invariant physical effects, and separate camera motion from environment change. We introduce EgoWAM, a controlled human-robot co-training framework that fixes the policy backbone, action head, and data mixture while varying only the world prediction target, comparing Pixel, DINO, and 3D motion flow. Across three real-world bimanual tasks, WAM co-training scales more effectively with in-the-wild egocentric human data than behavior cloning. Pixel-based prediction transfers weakly, while DINO and 3D flow yield substantial gains: DINO improves out-of-distribution object and scene generalization by up to 4x, and 3D flow improves in-domain performance by 20-30%. More details: https://gatech-rl2.github.io/egowam.github.io
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: world action models trained on egocentric human data for robot manipulation imitation learning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用第一人称人类数据训练世界动作模型进行机器人操控模仿学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用第一人称人类数据训练世界动作模型进行机器人操控模仿学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用第一人称人类数据训练世界动作模型进行机器人操控模仿学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: EgoWAM studies world action models for human-to-robot transfer using egocentric data, abstracting appearance and separating camera motion.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对人类演示数据中非可迁移因素（如形态、头部运动）影响机器人学习的问题，EgoWAM探索世界动作模型作为训练信号。提出可控的人-机器人联合训练框架，学习对智能体不变的世界表示。实验证明该方法能有效解耦场景变化与相机运动，提升迁移效果。这一工作为人数据驱动机器人学习提供了新范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对人类演示数据中非可迁移因素（如形态、头部运动）影响机器人学习的问题，EgoWAM探索世界动作模型作为训练信号。提出可控的人-机器人联合训练框架，学习对智能体不变的世界表示。实验证明该方法能有效解耦场景变化与相机运动，提升迁移效果。这一工作为人数据驱动机器人学习提供了新范式。

deep_dive.title: FlowDAgger: Human-in-the-Loop Adaptation of Generative Robot Policies in Latent Space
deep_dive.abstract: Pretrained generative robot policies based on flow matching and diffusion have achieved impressive results across a wide range of manipulation tasks. Yet real-world deployments routinely expose failure modes outside the pretraining distribution. Closing these gaps typically requires large-scale data collection or online reinforcement learning on physical hardware, which is impractical for rapid and safe adaptation. We present FlowDAgger, a sample- and compute-efficient method for adapting frozen generative robot policies from human interventions in latent space. Our key idea is action inversion: each human expert action is mapped to the noise that would have produced it under the frozen base policy, using reverse-time integration followed by local refinement. The resulting inverted noise provides supervision for a lightweight latent policy that steers the base model at deployment time, enabling rapid skill acquisition while preserving its behavioral priors. We evaluate FlowDAgger in simulation and on real-world bimanual and single-arm manipulation, adapting both action-head VLAs and world-action models from a handful of interventions. FlowDAgger outperforms supervised fine-tuning and latent-space RL baselines and preserves pretrained skills on held-out tasks, offering a practical path for adapting robot foundation models in the real world. Website: https://microsoft.github.io/FlowDAgger
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: adapts diffusion/flow-based generative robot policies using human feedback
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用人类反馈适应基于扩散/流的生成式机器人策略
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用人类反馈适应基于扩散/流的生成式机器人策略
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用人类反馈适应基于扩散/流的生成式机器人策略
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: FlowDAgger adapts frozen generative robot policies from human interventions via action inversion in latent space, avoiding costly online RL or large data collection.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 预训练的基于流匹配或扩散的生成式机器人策略在分布外时容易失败，而传统的在线强化学习或大规模数据收集不切实际。本文提出FlowDAgger，通过动作反演将人类干预映射到潜在噪声，利用反向时间积分在冻结基础策略下高效调整策略。实验表明该方法在少量干预下有效修复失败模式，为生成式策略的安全快速适应提供了新途径。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 预训练的基于流匹配或扩散的生成式机器人策略在分布外时容易失败，而传统的在线强化学习或大规模数据收集不切实际。本文提出FlowDAgger，通过动作反演将人类干预映射到潜在噪声，利用反向时间积分在冻结基础策略下高效调整策略。实验表明该方法在少量干预下有效修复失败模式，为生成式策略的安全快速适应提供了新途径。

deep_dive.title: SplatCtrl: Perception-Action Coupling via Gaussian Scene Representations and Reactive Robot Control
deep_dive.abstract: Robotic manipulators excel in structured environments but face substantial challenges in unstructured and dynamic settings. This paper presents SplatCtrl, a unified framework for real-time scene reconstruction and reactive robot motion generation to enable collision-free robotic arm control in previously unseen and continuously changing environments. Building on 3D Gaussian Splatting (3D-GS), we introduce a hybrid voxel-based filtering and dynamic Gaussian relocation strategy that supports efficient scene reconstruction from RGB-D streams while accommodating environmental changes. For safe and reactive control, we further propose a method for deriving continuous signed distance functions from isotropic Gaussians, providing stable and differentiable collision probability estimates that bridge classical distance fields with the modern implicit representation. These continuous distance metrics are incorporated into control barrier functions, resulting in a unified perception-action coupling framework that supports smooth and reliable real-time motion generation in response to scene changes. Experimental validation in simulation, on physical robot, and within shared human-robot workspace demonstrates the framework's effectiveness, achieving integrated scene reconstruction and reactive control in uncertain, and dynamic environments.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: real-time scene reconstruction and reactive robot control for dynamic environments
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 实时场景重建与反应式机器人控制，适用于动态环境
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 实时场景重建与反应式机器人控制，适用于动态环境
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 实时场景重建与反应式机器人控制，适用于动态环境
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: SplatCtrl unifies real-time scene reconstruction via 3D Gaussian Splatting with reactive collision-free robot arm control using continuous SDFs from Gaussians, enabling operation in dynamic environments.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有机器人在非结构化动态环境中表现不佳，因为缺乏实时场景感知和反应式控制。本文提出SplatCtrl框架，通过3D高斯散射实现RGB-D流的实时场景重建，并利用从各向同性高斯导出的连续符号距离函数进行安全反应式控制。实验表明该方法在未知和动态环境中实现了无碰撞操作，显著提升了机器人的环境适应能力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有机器人在非结构化动态环境中表现不佳，因为缺乏实时场景感知和反应式控制。本文提出SplatCtrl框架，通过3D高斯散射实现RGB-D流的实时场景重建，并利用从各向同性高斯导出的连续符号距离函数进行安全反应式控制。实验表明该方法在未知和动态环境中实现了无碰撞操作，显著提升了机器人的环境适应能力。

deep_dive.title: CLAP: Direct VLM-to-VLA Adaptation via Language-Action Grounding
deep_dive.abstract: Vision-language-action models (VLAs) inherit semantic capabilities from pretrained VLMs, yet large-scale post-training on robot data and architectural modifications can reshape the backbone so extensively that it becomes difficult to isolate what the VLM contributes to control. Directly converting pretrained VLMs into VLAs with minimal architectural change offers a more transparent path to understanding how VLM capabilities transfer across model scales. The core obstacle is output-distribution mismatch: predicting actions as bare numeric token sequences moves generation away from the VLM's pretrained language distribution, degrading the capabilities we seek to preserve. To address this, we propose CLAP (Causal Language-Action Prediction), which prepends each numeric action sequence with a natural-language action description, causally conditioning precise action-token prediction on a language-action plan without modifying the backbone architecture. With single-epoch fine-tuning alone, 2B CLAP achieves 90.8% on LIBERO (+14.9 pt over VLA-0) and improves robustness on LIBERO-PRO under language, object, and spatial perturbations. We will release CLAP at 0.8B, 2B, and 4B as an open-weight, multi-scale compact VLA family from a single VLM lineage, enabling controlled analysis of VLM-to-VLA capability transfer.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过语言-动作对齐实现VLM到VLA的适配
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过语言-动作对齐实现VLM到VLA的适配
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过语言-动作对齐实现VLM到VLA的适配
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: CLAP directly adapts pretrained VLMs to VLAs by prepending language tokens to action sequences, preserving language distribution for better control.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有VLA模型在大规模后训练中可能丢失VLM的语义能力。CLAP通过因果语言-动作预测，在动作序列前添加语言标记，使输出保持在语言分布内，从而保留VLM能力。实验表明该方法能有效迁移VLM能力到机器人控制，提升指令跟随和动作精度。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有VLA模型在大规模后训练中可能丢失VLM的语义能力。CLAP通过因果语言-动作预测，在动作序列前添加语言标记，使输出保持在语言分布内，从而保留VLM能力。实验表明该方法能有效迁移VLM能力到机器人控制，提升指令跟随和动作精度。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot

deep_dive.title: Learning More from Less: Reinforcement Learning from Hindsight
deep_dive.abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) is increasingly used to post-train vision-language-action (VLA) models, but every update consumes robot rollouts that are slow and costly to collect, making sample efficiency a central concern. Manipulation tasks typically provide only sparse rewards, so a weak policy fails almost every rollout early in training and has little to learn from, even when those failures execute coherent behavior. Such a failure, however, is a success at a different task. We present Learning from Hindsight (LfH), which brings hindsight relabeling to RL post-training of VLAs by scoring failed rollouts against the tasks they actually achieved. A single vision-language model relabels both the instruction and the reward, proposing a hindsight instruction for a group of failed rollouts and scoring how well each satisfies it, and the policy trains on the relabeled and original rollouts jointly. Because VLAs generalize across language, relabeling in language lets the policy learn more from the same trajectories. On out-of-distribution LIBERO-PRO tasks, where standard RL improves only slowly, LfH achieves $5\times$ improvement in sample efficiency, and outperforms a dense progress-reward baseline. The gains hold across VLA backbones and on a physical Franka robot.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: hindsight relabeling for RL post-training of VLA
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 后见回放用于VLA模型RL后训练
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 后见回放用于VLA模型RL后训练
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 后见回放用于VLA模型RL后训练
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Learning from Hindsight (LfH), applying hindsight relabeling to RL post-training of VLA models by treating failed rollouts as successes for simpler tasks, improving sample efficiency with a single vision-language model.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对VLA模型强化学习后训练中样本效率低的问题（大部分翻滚失败仅有稀疏奖励），该论文提出LfH，将后见回放引入该场景。它利用一个视觉语言模型为失败轨迹重标指令和奖励，使其成为其他任务的成功数据。实验表明，LfH显著提升了学习效率，在少量滚落内实现了策略改进。此法为VLA模型微调提供了更高效的方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对VLA模型强化学习后训练中样本效率低的问题（大部分翻滚失败仅有稀疏奖励），该论文提出LfH，将后见回放引入该场景。它利用一个视觉语言模型为失败轨迹重标指令和奖励，使其成为其他任务的成功数据。实验表明，LfH显著提升了学习效率，在少量滚落内实现了策略改进。此法为VLA模型微调提供了更高效的方法。

deep_dive.title: PAC-ACT: Post-training Actor-Critic for Action Chunking Transformers
deep_dive.abstract: Precision industrial contact manipulation requires reliable robot policies under pose perturbations and contact-force constraints. Vision-language-action models offer broad generalization but often introduce high inference latency and GPU-memory cost, while vision-action chunking policies are more suitable for real-time industrial control. However, these policies are usually trained by behavior cloning and suffer from distribution shift in contact-rich tasks. This paper proposes PAC-ACT, a reinforcement-learning post-training framework for pretrained Action Chunking Transformer policies. PAC-ACT reformulates policy optimization at the chunk level, constructs an ACT-transferred actor-critic architecture, and introduces a hybrid behavior-prior constraint to preserve the pretrained action distribution during online fine-tuning. Experiments on industrial precision-contact benchmarks show that PAC-ACT improves task success, contact stability, and force safety while retaining low latency and low GPU-memory usage. On the Contour task, PAC-ACT significantly reduces peak contact force and decreases the proportion of force readings above 60 N by 46 times. Sparse-reward ablations further show that the proposed behavior-prior constraint enables effective exploration under randomized initial poses.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: reinforcement learning post-training for action chunking transformer in robot control
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 对动作分块Transformer进行强化学习后训练用于机器人控制
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 对动作分块Transformer进行强化学习后训练用于机器人控制
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 对动作分块Transformer进行强化学习后训练用于机器人控制
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PAC-ACT applies reinforcement learning post-training to Action Chunking Transformers, using chunk-level policy optimization and actor-critic architecture to improve robustness in contact-rich tasks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对行为克隆训练的动作分块策略在接触密集任务中存在分布偏移的问题，本文提出PAC-ACT，一种强化学习后训练框架。它重新定义块级策略优化，构建ACT迁移的演员-评论家架构，并引入混合行为先验约束。在精密工业操作任务上，PAC-ACT显著提升了策略的鲁棒性和成功率。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对行为克隆训练的动作分块策略在接触密集任务中存在分布偏移的问题，本文提出PAC-ACT，一种强化学习后训练框架。它重新定义块级策略优化，构建ACT迁移的演员-评论家架构，并引入混合行为先验约束。在精密工业操作任务上，PAC-ACT显著提升了策略的鲁棒性和成功率。

deep_dive.title: Mosaic: Runtime-Efficient Multi-Agent Embodied Planning
deep_dive.abstract: LLM-based multi-agent embodied planning remains impractical due to prohibitively high execution latency. We identify failed actions as the dominant bottleneck, stemming from two core challenges: inaccurate state tracking under partial observability and inefficient coordination that produces redundant or conflicting actions. We introduce Mosaic, a runtime-efficient multi-agent planning framework that addresses both challenges. Mosaic maintains accurate yet lightweight state tracking through agent-centric semantic memory that stores objects in relative coordinates, enabling geometric transformations and coordination. It ensures efficient coordination through Integer Linear Programming that allocates actions at every planning step, enforcing physical feasibility and inter-agent coordination constraints. Across AI2-THOR and search-and-rescue benchmarks, Mosaic achieves 27-32% faster execution, 30-33% fewer LLM calls, 25-31% fewer steps, and 4-10% points higher success rates. These results demonstrate that efficient memory and constraint-guided coordination are critical for scalable, low-latency multi-agent planning.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multi-agent embodied planning using LLMs for task planning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用大语言模型进行多智能体具身任务规划
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用大语言模型进行多智能体具身任务规划
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用大语言模型进行多智能体具身任务规划
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Mosaic proposes a runtime-efficient framework for multi-agent embodied planning using LLMs, addressing state tracking and coordination via semantic memory and integer linear programming.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多智能体具身规划中执行延迟高的问题，Mosaic提出了一种运行时高效的框架。该方法通过智能体中心的语义记忆进行状态跟踪，并利用整数线性规划实现高效协调。实验表明，该框架显著减少了失败动作和规划时间。这一工作为具身多智能体系统的实际部署提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对多智能体具身规划中执行延迟高的问题，Mosaic提出了一种运行时高效的框架。该方法通过智能体中心的语义记忆进行状态跟踪，并利用整数线性规划实现高效协调。实验表明，该框架显著减少了失败动作和规划时间。这一工作为具身多智能体系统的实际部署提供了新思路。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: foundation model for robot task planning

quick_skim.title: Adapting Generalist Robot Policies with Semantic Reinforcement Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Generalist robot policies learn a diverse repertoire of behaviors from large-scale pretraining. In principle, this makes them excellent priors for downstream adaptation via reinforcement learning (RL). In practice, however, standard RL methods leveraging this prior optimize directly over robot actions, requiring the base policy's action distribution to be close to that of a performant policy from the start. This assumption breaks down for complex or long-horizon tasks that fall outside the pretraining distribution. Our key insight is that, for sufficiently expressive generalist policies, language prompts are an effective alternative space for learning to solve such tasks: modulating language inputs elicits skills already within the policy's repertoire, which can be composed to solve tasks beyond its zero-shot capabilities. We propose Semantic Action Reinforcement Learning (SARL), which learns to optimize this prompt space through online interaction, treating the generalist policy as a controllable skill prior. Importantly, leveraging pretrained skills rather than learning new ones from scratch yields structured, semantically meaningful exploration and highly efficient online improvement, and learning to modulate prompts through experience grounds them in induced real-world behaviors for robust task-solving. Across real-world settings and simulated benchmarks, we show SARL unlocks fundamentally new capabilities -- adapting VLA behavior to solve complex, long-horizon tasks -- and significantly outperforms existing approaches for improving robot behavior in deployment.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: reinforcement learning for adapting generalist robot policies
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用强化学习适应通用机器人策略
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用强化学习适应通用机器人策略
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用强化学习适应通用机器人策略
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes to adapt generalist robot policies via RL in language prompt space, leveraging the policy's existing skills learned from pretraining, outperforming direct action optimization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 标准强化学习直接优化动作要求基策略动作分布接近最优，对于复杂长时任务难以成立。本文提出在语言提示空间中使用强化学习来适应通用机器人策略，利用预训练策略已有的技能，通过优化语言输入来引出合适行为。在复杂和长时任务中，该方法优于直接动作优化，为通用策略的适应性提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 标准强化学习直接优化动作要求基策略动作分布接近最优，对于复杂长时任务难以成立。本文提出在语言提示空间中使用强化学习来适应通用机器人策略，利用预训练策略已有的技能，通过优化语言输入来引出合适行为。在复杂和长时任务中，该方法优于直接动作优化，为通用策略的适应性提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: Last-Meter Precision Navigation for UAVs: A Diffusion-Refined Aerial Visual Servoing Approach
quick_skim.abstract: In this work, we study the last-meter precision navigation for UAVs, e.g., autonomously reaching a target within the final 10 meters using monocular vision. This task is challenging due to scale ambiguity, rotation discontinuities, and the need for fine-grained spatial reasoning. Existing methods often fail under large viewpoint changes or lack generalization to unseen environments. To this end, we propose DreamNav, a coarse-to-fine diffusion-refined aerial visual servoing framework. In the first coarse-estimation stage, a robust regression policy employs a trigonometric parameterization to predict rotation by jointly modeling sine and cosine components, effectively mitigating optimization instabilities caused by angular periodicity. Given this coarse estimate, the second diffusion-refined stage utilizes a pre-trained world model to simulate future visual observations for candidate actions, selecting the trajectory that minimizes visual discrepancy with the target through a process of visual imagination. To support rigorous evaluation, we contribute PairUAV, a large-scale benchmark comprising 4.8 million image pairs across 72 scenes, curated from the University-1652 dataset. Extensive experiments show DreamNav outperforms strong visual servoing and foundation model baselines in accuracy and generalization, with zero-shot transfer to unseen scenes.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: diffusion-based coarse-to-fine visual servoing for UAV precision navigation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于扩散的粗到精视觉伺服用于无人机精确导航
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于扩散的粗到精视觉伺服用于无人机精确导航
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于扩散的粗到精视觉伺服用于无人机精确导航
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: DreamNav uses a diffusion-refined coarse-to-fine aerial visual servoing framework for last-meter precision UAV navigation under challenging conditions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 无人机最后一米精确导航面临尺度模糊和旋转不连续等挑战。DreamNav提出粗到精扩散精炼视觉伺服框架：粗估计阶段用三角函数参数化预测旋转，避免优化不稳定；精炼阶段利用扩散模型生成高精度控制信号。该方法提升了在未见环境中的泛化能力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 无人机最后一米精确导航面临尺度模糊和旋转不连续等挑战。DreamNav提出粗到精扩散精炼视觉伺服框架：粗估计阶段用三角函数参数化预测旋转，避免优化不稳定；精炼阶段利用扩散模型生成高精度控制信号。该方法提升了在未见环境中的泛化能力。

quick_skim.title: ORCAID: Oblique Rule-Based Continuous-Action Interpretation for Deep RL Policies
quick_skim.abstract: Explainability remains a key issue in reinforcement learning (RL). Distilling an interpretable policy from an agent trained in a complex environment is particularly challenging when the action space is continuous. We introduce ORCAID, a novel method for extracting interpretable rule-based policies from RL agents operating in mixed continuous-discrete environments with continuous action spaces. Our main contribution is an efficient oblique decision tree training algorithm that partitions the state space by hyperplanes and fits local linear models. The key idea lies in a three-stage split search: efficient random initialization, local refinement, and backward elimination. Finally, adjacent leaves are merged to yield a concise set of interpretable rules describing a given deep RL policy. We evaluate ORCAID across multiple RL environments, demonstrating that the extracted rule-based policies maintain strong performance with a low number of parameters and can even be used to improve the performance of the original deep RL policy.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: rule extraction for continuous-action RL policies interpretability
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 从连续动作强化学习策略中提取可解释规则
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 从连续动作强化学习策略中提取可解释规则
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 从连续动作强化学习策略中提取可解释规则
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ORCAID extracts interpretable oblique decision trees from RL agents with continuous action spaces, enabling policy understanding without sacrificing performance.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 强化学习策略的可解释性仍是关键问题。针对连续动作空间，ORCAID提出一种高效的斜决策树训练算法，通过随机初始化、局部精炼和反向消除三阶段分裂搜索，并融合相邻叶子节点，从深度RL策略中提取简洁的可解释规则集。该方法在混合连续离散环境中有效平衡了准确性与可解释性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 强化学习策略的可解释性仍是关键问题。针对连续动作空间，ORCAID提出一种高效的斜决策树训练算法，通过随机初始化、局部精炼和反向消除三阶段分裂搜索，并融合相邻叶子节点，从深度RL策略中提取简洁的可解释规则集。该方法在混合连续离散环境中有效平衡了准确性与可解释性。

quick_skim.title: Freeform Preference Learning for Robotic Manipulation
quick_skim.abstract: Reward design remains a central bottleneck for autonomous robot policy improvement, especially in long-horizon manipulation tasks where sparse success labels provide too little signal and binary preferences collapse many competing notions of quality into one ambiguous signal. We introduce Freeform Preference Learning (FPL), a method for learning robot policies from freeform human preferences. Rather than asking annotators which of two trajectories is better overall, FPL lets them define natural-language preference axes, such as speed, safety, quality of placement, or carefulness, and provide pairwise preferences along each axis. These annotations are used to learn a language-conditioned reward model that maps a trajectory and preference label to an axis-specific reward. We use this model to train a reward-conditioned policy that optimizes across the multiple human-specified dimensions. Across four real-world and two simulated long-horizon manipulation tasks, FPL improves over sparse-reward and binary-preference methods by 38 percentage points. Beyond improved performance, FPL learns dense progress signals without explicit subtask segmentation, shows compositionality of behavior not present in the data, and allows users to steer the policy towards different behaviors at test time without retraining. Blog post with videos available at https://freeform-pl.github.io/fpl.website/
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: freeform preference learning for robot manipulation RL
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于机器人操作强化学习的自由形式偏好学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于机器人操作强化学习的自由形式偏好学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于机器人操作强化学习的自由形式偏好学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Freeform Preference Learning (FPL) learns robot policies from natural-language preference axes, enabling rich reward signals for long-horizon manipulation tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对机器人操作中奖励设计瓶颈，提出自由形式偏好学习（FPL），允许标注者提供沿自然语言偏好轴（如速度、安全性）的成对比较，从而学习语言条件奖励模型。FPL显著改善了长周期操作任务的策略学习，在仿真和真实机器人上均取得较好效果。该方法为机器人从人类偏好中学习提供了更灵活、信息更丰富的框架。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对机器人操作中奖励设计瓶颈，提出自由形式偏好学习（FPL），允许标注者提供沿自然语言偏好轴（如速度、安全性）的成对比较，从而学习语言条件奖励模型。FPL显著改善了长周期操作任务的策略学习，在仿真和真实机器人上均取得较好效果。该方法为机器人从人类偏好中学习提供了更灵活、信息更丰富的框架。

quick_skim.title: PRISM: Personalized Robotic Dataset Generation via Image-based Scene and Motion Synthesis
quick_skim.abstract: Recent advances in large-scale pretrained vision-language-action models have improved robot policy learning, but directly deploying such policies in user-specific environments remains challenging due to limited generalization, which inevitably requires collecting a dataset tailored to the target environment. Teleoperation yields well-aligned data but is costly and difficult to scale, whereas simulation scales easily but struggles to resemble the target environment and generate task-specific trajectories. To meet both simultaneously, we propose PRISM, an end-to-end pipeline that generates personalized robotic datasets from a single image and a natural-language instruction. PRISM constructs digital cousin scenes that are semantically and geometrically aligned with the user environment yet diverse at the instance level, and synthesizes executable demonstrations without human teleoperation. Extensive experiments show that policies trained on PRISM-generated datasets outperform those trained on baseline-generated datasets on LIBERO and LIBERO-Plus, achieve up to 100\% success rate on three real-world manipulation tasks, and maintain stronger performance when evaluated in environments that differ from those seen during training.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: personalized dataset generation for robot learning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 个性化数据集生成用于机器人学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 个性化数据集生成用于机器人学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 个性化数据集生成用于机器人学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes PRISM, an end-to-end pipeline that generates personalized robotic datasets from a single image and language instruction by constructing digital cousin scenes and synthesizing task-specific trajectories, enabling efficient policy adaptation to user environments.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对预训练VLA模型在用户特定环境中泛化有限的问题，该论文提出PRISM，一种端到端管线，可从一张图像和自然语言指令生成个性化机器人数据集。它通过构造语义类似的数字孪生场景并合成任务轨迹，避免了昂贵的遥操作数据收集，同时保证场景真实性和任务相关性。实验证明，使用PRISM生成的数据微调策略可显著提升在新环境中的成功率。PRISM为机器人策略的快速个性化部署提供了实用工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对预训练VLA模型在用户特定环境中泛化有限的问题，该论文提出PRISM，一种端到端管线，可从一张图像和自然语言指令生成个性化机器人数据集。它通过构造语义类似的数字孪生场景并合成任务轨迹，避免了昂贵的遥操作数据收集，同时保证场景真实性和任务相关性。实验证明，使用PRISM生成的数据微调策略可显著提升在新环境中的成功率。PRISM为机器人策略的快速个性化部署提供了实用工具。

quick_skim.title: HumAIN: Human-Aware Implicit Social Robot Navigation
quick_skim.abstract: Effective social robot navigation requires sensitivity to human behavior, often revealed through subtle skeletal cues like gait and orientation. We present Human-Aware Implicit Social Robot Navigation (HumAIN), a novel framework that fuses implicit social cues directly into the planning loop via knowledge distillation. We first employ a transformer-based teacher model that fuses rich multi-modal inputs, including historic images, skeletal keypoints, robot state, and a robot's target goal, to learn robust, human-aware representations for the robot's future trajectory planning. To enable real-time deployment, we then distill this knowledge into a lightweight student model. By optimizing for both trajectory reconstruction and latent feature alignment with the teacher, the student learns to infer complex social dynamics from minimal inputs. Bridging the prediction-planning gap with an efficient distilled architecture, our method enables robots to reason about human behavior in a manner that is adaptive, robust, and socially compliant. We validate HumAIN through extensive experiments, where it improves trajectory prediction metrics by an average of 29.8% across all metrics compared to state-of-the-art baselines. These results highlight the benefit of using implicit, whole-body cues to achieve human-like navigation awareness on resource-constrained platforms.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: human-aware social robot navigation with implicit cues
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用隐式社交线索的人感知机器人导航
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用隐式社交线索的人感知机器人导航
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用隐式社交线索的人感知机器人导航
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: HumAIN fuses implicit social cues (skeletal keypoints, gait) into robot navigation via a transformer teacher and lightweight student distillation, enabling human-aware trajectory planning in real-time.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 提出HumAIN框架，通过知识蒸馏将隐式社交线索（骨架关键点、步态等）融入机器人导航规划。采用Transformer教师模型融合多模态输入学习人感知表示，再蒸馏为轻量级学生模型实现实时部署。优化轨迹重建和潜在特征对齐，使机器人在动态环境中表现出社交感知的导航行为。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 提出HumAIN框架，通过知识蒸馏将隐式社交线索（骨架关键点、步态等）融入机器人导航规划。采用Transformer教师模型融合多模态输入学习人感知表示，再蒸馏为轻量级学生模型实现实时部署。优化轨迹重建和潜在特征对齐，使机器人在动态环境中表现出社交感知的导航行为。

quick_skim.title: Learning Generalizable Skill Policy with Data-Efficient Unsupervised RL
quick_skim.abstract: Unsupervised Reinforcement Learning (URL) aims to pre-train scalable, skill-conditioned policies without extrinsic rewards, serving as a foundation for downstream control tasks. Despite recent progress, we argue that current off-policy URL methods are limited by two critical, overlooked bottlenecks: (1) non-stationary skill semantics and (2) brittle generalization. To address these challenges, we propose GenDa (Generalizable Data-efficient Agent), a unified framework for robust unsupervised reinforcement learning. First, we introduce a skill relabeling mechanism to mitigate non-stationarity and significantly improve data efficiency for pre-training. Second, we propose a Complementary Information Bottleneck (CIB), encouraging the learned skill policy to focus on ego-centric features and become robust to distribution shifts for downstream tasks. Through various experiments, we demonstrate that GenDa significantly enhances the scalability of URL with superior generalizability and data efficiency. Our code and videos are available at https://ihatebroccoli.github.io/official-GenDa.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: unsupervised RL for skill policy pre-training, downstream robot control
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 无监督强化学习预训练技能策略，用于下游机器人控制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 无监督强化学习预训练技能策略，用于下游机器人控制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 无监督强化学习预训练技能策略，用于下游机器人控制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes GenDa, a unified framework with skill relabeling and Complementary Information Bottleneck for data-efficient unsupervised RL, improving generalization for downstream robot control.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对当前无监督强化学习方法在技能语义非平稳和泛化脆弱的问题，提出GenDa框架，通过技能重标注机制和互补信息瓶颈，实现数据高效的技能策略预训练，显著提升下游机器人控制任务的泛化性能。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对当前无监督强化学习方法在技能语义非平稳和泛化脆弱的问题，提出GenDa框架，通过技能重标注机制和互补信息瓶颈，实现数据高效的技能策略预训练，显著提升下游机器人控制任务的泛化性能。

quick_skim.title: GaP: A Graph-as-Policy Multi-Agent Self-Learning Harness For Variational Automation Tasks
quick_skim.abstract: For robots to work reliably in commercial and industrial applications, can recent advances in agentic coding systems combine interpretable robot programming with the open-world adaptability of model-free policies? We focus on "Variational Automation" (VA), a class of tasks that have larger variations in object geometry and pose than fixed automation. Model-free policies often struggle to close the reliability gap for VA tasks, which must be executed persistently and reliably in commercial and industrial applications. Motivated by prior work on Task and Motion Planning (TAMP) and the Robot Operating System (ROS), we introduce Graph-as-Policy (GaP), a multi-agent coding harness that generates directed computation graphs with perception, planning, and control nodes from a Modular Open Robot Skill Library (MORSL). GaP then generates an internal simulation environment to rehearse task instances with different graphs in parallel to iteratively refine the graph structure and parameters to improve success rates and throughput. Evaluation with 8 new open VA task benchmarks, 4 in-simulation and 4 in real-world, suggests that GaP can achieve success rates that significantly outperform baselines. Details, code, and data can be found online: https://graph-robots.github.io/gap
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: graph-as-policy for multi-agent task and motion planning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 图作为策略的多智能体任务与运动规划
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 图作为策略的多智能体任务与运动规划
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 图作为策略的多智能体任务与运动规划
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: GaP generates directed computation graphs for multi-agent task and motion planning, combining interpretability of TAMP with adaptability of model-free policies.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 变体自动任务需要可靠性与适应性。GaP提出图策略框架，生成有向计算图，结合任务与运动规划的明确性和无模型策略的灵活性，支持多智能体协作，在工业场景中可靠性大幅提升。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 变体自动任务需要可靠性与适应性。GaP提出图策略框架，生成有向计算图，结合任务与运动规划的明确性和无模型策略的灵活性，支持多智能体协作，在工业场景中可靠性大幅提升。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: foundation model for robot task planning

quick_skim.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning-Empowered Wireless Sensor Networking for 6G Closed-Loop Controls
quick_skim.abstract: Robots are increasingly deployed in remote or hazardous areas for mission-critical control tasks. Due to their limited individual capabilities, they have to rely on other field sensors to obtain the state information of targets, and also a dedicated edge information hub (EIH) to enable information exchange, sensing data analysis and control command generation. Such configuration follows a sensing-communication-computing-control (SC3) closed loop. To optimize the whole closed-loop performance, this paper minimizes the linear quadratic regulator (LQR) control cost by designing the sensor-to-EIH bandwidth allocation. Specifically, we first model the distortion noise caused by limited communication data rate based on the mutual information theory. Next, under the control policy based on the Kalman filter and LQR controller, we formulate the control process as a partially observable Markov decision process (POMDP), and develop a deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based sensor-to-EIH bandwidth allocation scheme. The proximal policy optimization (PPO) algorithm is utilized to train the DRL agent. Simulation results are provided to show the superiority of the proposed DRL-based scheme.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Deep RL for robot control via wireless communication optimization
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 深度强化学习优化无线通信以支持机器人控制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 深度强化学习优化无线通信以支持机器人控制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 深度强化学习优化无线通信以支持机器人控制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Optimizes bandwidth allocation for robot-in-the-loop 6G control using deep RL, minimizing LQR cost.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对机器人依靠无线传感器和边缘中心进行闭环控制的场景，提出使用深度强化学习优化传感器到边缘中心的带宽分配，以最小化线性二次型调节器控制代价。核心方法基于互信息建模通信失真噪声，并设计RL策略分配带宽。实验验证了该方法在降低控制成本方面的有效性，为机器人远程闭环控制提供了通信感知的优化方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对机器人依靠无线传感器和边缘中心进行闭环控制的场景，提出使用深度强化学习优化传感器到边缘中心的带宽分配，以最小化线性二次型调节器控制代价。核心方法基于互信息建模通信失真噪声，并设计RL策略分配带宽。实验验证了该方法在降低控制成本方面的有效性，为机器人远程闭环控制提供了通信感知的优化方案。

quick_skim.title: From World Models to World Action Models: A Concise Tutorial for Robotics
quick_skim.abstract: World models are increasingly used in embodied intelligence and generative simulation, yet their scope remains ambiguous across communities. This tutorial presents a design-space view of world models as action-conditioned predictive models that estimate the future evolution of task-relevant observations or states. We categorize existing methods into observation-space and state-space world models, comparing their trade-offs in visual fidelity, spatial structure, physical interpretability, and control usability. We further introduce world action models, which connect predicted futures with executable robot actions, and summarize four representative paradigms: imagine-then-execute, video-feature-conditioned action prediction, joint video-action modeling, and auxiliary video prediction for policy learning. The goal of this tutorial is to clarify the conceptual scope of world (action) models and provide a structured taxonomy for embodied prediction and control.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: tutorial on world models for embodied AI
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: A tutorial presenting world models and world action models for robotics, covering design-space and paradigms for task-relevant prediction and action generation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 当前世界模型在具身智能领域定义模糊，本教程系统梳理了世界模型的设计空间，将其分为观测空间和状态空间两类，并进一步提出世界动作模型，连接预测的未来与可执行的机器人动作。总结了四种代表性范式，为机器人任务规划与决策提供了统一的参考框架。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 当前世界模型在具身智能领域定义模糊，本教程系统梳理了世界模型的设计空间，将其分为观测空间和状态空间两类，并进一步提出世界动作模型，连接预测的未来与可执行的机器人动作。总结了四种代表性范式，为机器人任务规划与决策提供了统一的参考框架。

quick_skim.title: Learning 4D Geometric Priors for Inference-Efficient World Action Models
quick_skim.abstract: World Action Models (WAMs) have shown strong potential for robotic manipulation by jointly modeling visual future dynamics and executable action sequences. However, existing video-action co-training methods primarily optimize appearance-oriented video latents, which may insufficiently capture the temporally evolving geometry required for precise manipulation. We propose MECo-WAM, a Multi-Expert Co-Training World Action Model that injects action-relevant 4D geometric priors into video-action representations while preserving the original lightweight inference graph. During training, MECo-WAM combines video and action experts with a lightweight 4D expert supervised by relational targets from a frozen VGGT encoder. Asymmetric expert visibility prevents non-causal shortcuts from auxiliary geometry to action generation. To transfer geometric knowledge into the deployed video-action pathway, we introduce decayed 4D read-mask attention, which provides restricted current-frame geometric guidance early in training and progressively removes this dependency. We further propose action-aware temporal geometric distillation, which aligns within-frame geometric relations and their temporal evolution while emphasizing visual regions most relevant to robot actions. At deployment, all auxiliary 4D components are removed. Experiments on LIBERO (98.2%), RoboTwin 2.0 (92.6%), and challenging real-world manipulation tasks show that MECo-WAM improves manipulation performance without increasing inference cost.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: world action models with 4D geometric priors for robotic manipulation perception
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 带有4D几何先验的世界动作模型用于机器人操控感知
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 带有4D几何先验的世界动作模型用于机器人操控感知
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 带有4D几何先验的世界动作模型用于机器人操控感知
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MECo-WAM injects 4D geometric priors into world action models for robotic manipulation, improving precision while maintaining lightweight inference.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有世界动作模型主要优化外观潜在表示而忽略时空几何的问题，MECo-WAM提出多专家联合训练框架。通过引入轻量4D专家网络，注入动作相关的几何先验。实验表明该方法在操控任务中提升了动作预测精度。这一工作强调了几何信息在动作模型中的重要性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有世界动作模型主要优化外观潜在表示而忽略时空几何的问题，MECo-WAM提出多专家联合训练框架。通过引入轻量4D专家网络，注入动作相关的几何先验。实验表明该方法在操控任务中提升了动作预测精度。这一工作强调了几何信息在动作模型中的重要性。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: foundation models for robot perception and manipulation
